learning_outcomes.description: Conduct patient histories, comprehensive oral examinations and recognise and diagnose a broad range of conditions of the oral cavity and proximate structures.
Develop clinical reasoning and judgement skills to facilitate comprehensive oral care planning, considering individual patient's expectations and needs.
Apply scientific evidence to justify the design of comprehensive oral care plans (including referrals) to manage individuals diagnosed with conditions of the oral cavity and proximate structures.

Design and implement preventive care programs to improve and maintain oral health and to minimise risk of future disease in individuals diagnosed with dental caries, non-carious tooth surface loss and/or periodontal disease.
Apply principles of minimum intervention to cavity preparations for intra-coronal restorations and the management of carious and non-carious disease of dental hard tissue.
Demonstrate clinical skills to debride teeth effectively for patients with varying degrees of periodontal disease (PSR ≥ 4).

Design strategies relevant to clinical dental practice to develop personal and professional leadership qualities, including resilience to work with possible health system challenges in delivering culturally safe health care.
Employ critical reflection to experiential learning in both the pre-clinical and clinical environment, recognising advances in personal knowledge, understanding and clinical ability.
Demonstrate exemplary verbal communication with patients, carers, clinical staff and peers, and create accurate written communication.
Recognising limitations in knowledge, understanding and clinical skills, justify further professional development opportunities and refer within the inter-professional health team.

assessments.task_description: The mid-semester quiz is designed to assess students’ understanding and application of the knowledge acquired throughout the course. The assessment may include a combination of multiple-choice questions and short-answer questions, covering key concepts, principles, and clinical applications discussed during lectures and other learning activities.

assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

assessments.task_description: The viva voce examination constitutes a significant component of the overall course grade (30%). This assessment is designed to evaluate students’ ability to demonstrate and communicate their diagnostic reasoning, clinical judgement, and treatment planning skills to a panel of examiners. Students will be provided with a clinical case record for review and analysis prior to the examination. During the viva voce, they will be required to present their findings, justify their diagnoses and treatment decisions, and respond to questions from the examiners.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.late_submission: You will receive a mark of 0 if this assessment is submitted late.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

assessments.task_description:
For further information, including instructions for each part and specific due dates, please see the Blackboard Task Sheet for this assessment item.

In this task, students are required to complete and submit a Clinical Practice Portfolio over the course of the semester, in order to document clinical performance within the patient clinic. Students must complete the portfolio for ALL clinical, dental assisting, fail to attend, and observational sessions. It consists of multiple ‘parts’ that all contribute to an overall ‘Pass’ or ‘Fail’ for the assessment at the end of the semester.

This is a hurdle requirement and must be passed to progress throughout the Doctor of Dental Medicine.

In accordance with PPL Assessment Procedure Section 3 Part J, supplementary assessment will NOT be available for clinical practice performance (portfolio) as professional competencies are developed over a 16 week practicum.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Submissions occur via submission links on the course Blackboard site.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: You will receive a mark of 0 if this assessment is submitted late.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description: The end of semester examination is a MUST PASS examination during the examination period. The examination may be structured to include multiple choice, short-answer questions, case scenarios and long-answer questions on any of the following subjects: Dentomaxillofacial Radiology, Special Needs Dentistry, Oral Surgery, Periodontics and Oral Medicine. The examination assesses knowledge and understanding across all course materials, including lecture content, textbook material, research articles, and relevant clinical material.
